title: Add insecureAcceptAnything to default docker-daemon transport

comments.body: Yeah, as #212 says, this is blocked on~~, or should be a part of,~~ https://github.com/containers/image/pull/79
@aweiteka what is the state of this?

AFAICS now that `docker-daemon:` is in master, this should work, and be mergeable, after a rebase. An integration test would also be nice but I have no idea how to conveniently integration-test `docker-daemon` ATM anyway, so let’s not block on that.
This isn't impacting the Docker integration right? Afaict Docker does not need this field from the policy when verifying on pull

No, this is “push”.  And, really, in the default configuration, redundant with the global default which is the same—but this means that _if_ the user restricts the global default, push is still allowed unless they explicitly restrict it as well.
